title: 10 best herbal teas for hemorrhoids
intro_desc: Herbal teas for hemorrhoids are infusions made from various plants that can help alleviate the symptoms of hemorrhoids, such as inflammation, pain, and itching. These teas typically contain anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties that can help reduce swelling and provide relief. Some examples of herbal teas that are beneficial for hemorrhoids include chamomile, witch hazel, and calendula. Chamomile tea is known for its anti-inflammatory properties, while witch hazel and calendula have astringent and healing effects. Drinking these teas can improve the lives of individuals suffering from hemorrhoids by reducing their discomfort and promoting healing, allowing them to engage in daily activities without pain or irritation.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Horse chestnut tea helps with hemorrhoids because it contains a compound called aescin, which has anti-inflammatory and vasoconstrictive properties. These properties can help reduce swelling, pain, and discomfort associated with hemorrhoids. Additionally, horse chestnut tea is rich in flavonoids, which have been shown to improve venous tone and reduce capillary permeability, further alleviating symptoms of hemorrhoids. Drinking horse chestnut tea may also help improve circulation and strengthen the walls of blood vessels, promoting overall vascular health and reducing the risk of developing hemorrhoids. However, it is important to consult with a healthcare provider before starting any new herbal remedy.
remedy_properties: Venotonic: Horse chestnut tea helps improve circulation and strengthens the walls of veins, which can reduce the swelling and discomfort associated with hemorrhoids (active constituents
Anti-inflammatory: The anti-inflammatory properties of horse chestnut tea can help alleviate pain and inflammation in hemorrhoids (active constituents
Antioxidant: Horse chestnut tea contains antioxidants that can help protect blood vessels and reduce oxidative stress, which may contribute to hemorrhoid development (active constituents
Astringent: Horse chestnut tea has astringent properties that can help tighten and tone tissues, reducing symptoms such as bleeding and itching in hemorrhoids (active constituents
Analgesic: Horse chestnut tea may help alleviate pain associated with hemorrhoids due to its analgesic properties (active constituents
remedy_parts: Seeds: The seeds of the horse chestnut tea plant contain escin, a saponin that helps to reduce inflammation and strengthen blood vessels, making it effective for treating hemorrhoids.
Bark: The bark of the horse chestnut tea plant contains aescin and other flavonoids that have anti-inflammatory and vasoconstrictor properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of hemorrhoids.
Leaves: The leaves of the horse chestnut tea plant contain flavonoids and tannins that have astringent properties, which can help reduce swelling and irritation in hemorrhoids.
remedy_recipe: In a pot, combine 2 tablespoons of dried horse chestnut bark with 8 ounces of water.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce heat and let it simmer for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the tea to remove the horse chestnut bark, then let it cool down to a drinkable temperature.
Drink 1-2 cups of the tea daily, preferably before meals.
Discard any leftover tea after 24 hours and prepare a fresh batch for the next day.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Kinnikinnick tea helps with hemorrhoids because it contains anti-inflammatory and astringent properties, which can help reduce swelling, pain, and bleeding in the affected area. The herbal tea is made from the bark and leaves of the kinnikinnick plant, which has been traditionally used by Native Americans to treat various health conditions, including hemorrhoids. The tannins present in kinnikinnick tea can help shrink the swollen veins and promote healing, providing relief from the discomfort and pain caused by hemorrhoids. Moreover, kinnikinnick tea is caffeine-free and can be consumed regularly to manage the symptoms of hemorrhoids.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Kinnikinnick tea has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and pain in hemorrhoids, thanks to the presence of tannins and flavonoids.
Astringent: Its astringent properties help shrink and tighten tissues, reducing bleeding and promoting healing, due to the presence of tannins.

Analgesic: Kinnikinnick tea contains analgesic compounds that help alleviate pain and discomfort associated with hemorrhoids, which can be attributed to several active constituents, including alkaloids.
Antimicrobial: The antimicrobial properties of kinnikinnick tea help prevent infections in hemorrhoids, ensuring a faster recovery, and are attributed to compounds like terpenes and alkaloids.

Antioxidant: Its high antioxidant content supports overall vascular health, reducing the risk of hemorrhoids and aiding in their treatment, as antioxidants like flavonoids help protect blood vessels from damage.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the kinnikinnick tea plant have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce swelling and pain associated with hemorrhoids.
Leaves: The leaves of the plant contain tannins that can help constrict blood vessels, reducing bleeding and discomfort caused by hemorrhoids.
Bark: The bark of the kinnikinnick tea plant contains compounds that can help soothe irritated tissue and promote healing, making it useful for treating hemorrhoids.
Seeds: The seeds of the plant are rich in antioxidants that can help reduce inflammation and promote tissue repair, making them beneficial for hemorrhoid treatment.
remedy_recipe: In a pot, combine 1 tablespoon of dried oak, 1 tablespoon of dried sumac, and 2 cups of water, and bring the mixture to a boil.
Reduce heat, let it simmer for 15 minutes, then add 1 tablespoon of dried mullein leaves and let it steep for an additional 10 minutes.
Strain the tea to remove solid particles and discard the used herbs.
Allow the tea to cool down to a drinkable temperature.
Drink 2-3 cups of the kinnikinnick tea daily for hemorrhoid relief.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: White tea helps with hemorrhoids because it is rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling and irritation in the affected area. The tannins present in white tea are known to have astringent properties that can help constrict blood vessels, thereby reducing pain and inflammation. Additionally, white tea has antibacterial properties that can help prevent infections and promote healing. Drinking white tea or using it as a compress can provide relief from the symptoms of hemorrhoids, making it a natural and effective remedy for this common condition.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: White tea has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and pain in hemorrhoids, thanks to the presence of flavonoids and tannins.
Antioxidant: The high antioxidant content in white tea helps promote healing and reduces irritation in hemorrhoids by neutralizing free radicals.
Astringent: White tea's astringent properties, due to tannins, help constrict blood vessels and decrease inflammation, providing relief from hemorrhoid symptoms.
Antimicrobial: White tea's antimicrobial properties help prevent infections in hemorrhoids, as it contains catechins and polyphenols that inhibit bacterial growth.
Venotonic: White tea can strengthen vein walls and improve circulation, reducing the risk of hemorrhoids, due to its richness in rutin, a type of flavonoid.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in antioxidants, white tea leaves can help reduce inflammation and irritation in hemorrhoids.
Buds: High in tannins, white tea buds can aid in constricting blood vessels, which may help alleviate symptoms of hemorrhoids.
Bark: Containing anti-inflammatory properties, white tea bark can help soothe and reduce pain and swelling in hemorrhoids.
remedy_recipe: Boil 2 cups of water and add 1 teaspoon of dried white tea leaves, allowing them to steep for 5-7 minutes.
While the tea is steeping, finely chop 1 tablespoon of dried yarrow root and 1 tablespoon of dried calendula flowers.
After the tea has steeped, strain out the white tea leaves and add the chopped yarrow root and calendula flowers to the hot tea.
Cover the tea and let it sit for an additional 10-15 minutes to allow the herbs to infuse.
Strain out the herbs and drink the tea while it is still warm, up to three times a day for hemorrhoid relief.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Stoneroot tea helps with hemorrhoids because it contains anti-inflammatory and astringent properties that can help reduce swelling, pain, and bleeding associated with hemorrhoids. The herb's active compounds, such as tannins, can help shrink swollen veins and promote healing of the affected area. Additionally, stoneroot tea has been traditionally used to improve circulation and strengthen blood vessels, which can help prevent the development of hemorrhoids in the first place. Its soothing and calming effects can also help alleviate stress and anxiety, which can exacerbate hemorrhoid symptoms. Overall, stoneroot tea can provide a natural and holistic approach to managing hemorrhoids and promoting digestive health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Stoneroot tea can reduce inflammation, which is important for hemorrhoids as they are characterized by inflammation and swelling in the veins of the rectum or anus. The anti-inflammatory properties come from the presence of triterpene saponins, such as oleanolic acid and ursolic acid.

Astringent: Stoneroot tea has astringent properties that can help shrink and tighten tissue, which can provide relief for hemorrhoids. The astringent properties are attributed to the tannins present in stoneroot.
Analgesic: Stoneroot tea can help alleviate pain, which is a common symptom of hemorrhoids. The analgesic properties are due to the presence of various flavonoids, such as rutin and quercetin.

Antimicrobial: Stoneroot tea can help prevent infections, which is important for hemorrhoids as they can be prone to bacterial and fungal infections. The antimicrobial properties are attributed to the presence of phenolic acids and flavonoids.

Circulatory stimulant: Stoneroot tea can improve blood flow and reduce congestion in the pelvic area, which can help alleviate symptoms of hemorrhoids. The circulatory stimulant properties are due to the presence of essential oils, such as methyl salicylate and eugenol.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the stoneroot tea plant contain anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce swelling and pain associated with hemorrhoids.
Rhizomes: The rhizomes of the plant have astringent qualities that can help shrink tissue and alleviate symptoms of hemorrhoids.
Leaves: The leaves of the stoneroot tea plant are rich in tannins, which can help reduce inflammation and promote healing of hemorrhoids.
Flowers: The flowers of the plant contain flavonoids, which have antioxidant properties that can help improve circulation and reduce pain and inflammation in hemorrhoids.
Bark: The bark of the stoneroot tea plant contains compounds that can help reduce itching and irritation, providing relief for hemorrhoid symptoms.
remedy_recipe: In a quart pot, combine 1 ounce of dried stoneroot root with 4 cups of water, and bring to a boil.
Reduce heat and simmer the mixture, covered, for 20-30 minutes.
Strain the tea to remove the solids, and let it cool to a drinkable temperature.
Stir in honey or another sweetener to taste, if desired.
Drink 1 cup of the stoneroot tea, 3 times a day, for relief of hemorrhoid symptoms.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Horsetail tea helps with hemorrhoids because it has anti-inflammatory and astringent properties that can reduce swelling, pain, and bleeding in the affected area. The tea is rich in silica, which is known to strengthen blood vessels and connective tissues, promoting healing and preventing further damage. Additionally, horsetail tea has diuretic properties that can help alleviate symptoms of hemorrhoids by reducing fluid retention and inflammation in the rectal area. Its soothing and healing effects make horsetail tea an effective natural remedy for hemorrhoids.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Horsetail tea contains anti-inflammatory compounds, like flavonoids and phenolic acids, which help reduce inflammation and pain associated with hemorrhoids.
Astringent: The astringent properties of horsetail tea, due to tannins, help shrink swollen tissues, promoting healing and reducing bleeding in hemorrhoids.
Antimicrobial: Horsetail tea has antimicrobial properties, primarily from phenolic acids, which help prevent infections in hemorrhoids and speed up recovery.
Diuretic: Horsetail tea promotes diuresis, facilitating the removal of excess fluids and reducing swelling in hemorrhoids, thanks to the presence of equisetin and other saponins.
Collagen synthesis: Horsetail tea contains silica and other nutrients that support collagen synthesis, essential for maintaining healthy connective tissues and promoting wound healing in hemorrhoids.
remedy_parts: Rhizomes: The underground stem of the horsetail tea plant is rich in silica, which can help strengthen blood vessels and reduce inflammation, making it useful for treating hemorrhoids.
Leaves: The leaves of the horsetail tea plant contain flavonoids and tannins, which have astringent properties that can help reduce swelling and pain associated with hemorrhoids.
Stems: The stems of the horsetail tea plant contain high amounts of silica and other minerals, which can help promote healing and tissue regeneration, making it beneficial for hemorrhoids.
remedy_recipe: In a pot, boil 2 cups of water and add 2 tablespoons of dried horsetail herb, then let it simmer for 10-15 minutes.
After simmering, strain the tea to remove the herb and allow it to cool down to a comfortable temperature.
Add raw honey to taste, if desired, for added anti-inflammatory benefits and improved taste.
Drink 2-3 cups of the horsetail tea daily, between meals, for at least a week to reduce symptoms of hemorrhoids.
Store any leftover tea in the refrigerator for up to 2 days, reheating as needed.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Meadowsweet tea helps with hemorrhoids because it contains salicylic acid, a compound similar to aspirin that has anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties. Hemorrhoids are swollen veins in the rectum or anus that can cause pain, itching, and bleeding. The anti-inflammatory properties of meadowsweet tea can help reduce swelling and irritation in the affected area, providing relief from these symptoms. Additionally, the astringent properties of meadowsweet tea may help to tighten and tone the tissues, reducing the likelihood of hemorrhoids flare-ups. Drinking meadowsweet tea regularly may help to alleviate the discomfort and pain associated with hemorrhoids, making it a natural and effective remedy for this common condition.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Meadowsweet tea contains salicylates, which help reduce inflammation, providing relief from the swelling and pain associated with hemorrhoids.
Astringent: Tannins present in meadowsweet tea tighten and constrict irritated blood vessels, reducing bleeding and promoting healing of hemorrhoids.
Analgesic: The salicylates in meadowsweet tea provide pain-relieving effects, helping to alleviate the discomfort caused by hemorrhoids.
Antimicrobial: Meadowsweet tea has antimicrobial properties due to its tannin and flavonoid content, which help prevent infection and promote tissue repair in hemorrhoids.
Anti-diarrheal: Meadowsweet tea can help regulate bowel movements, reducing the strain on hemorrhoids and promoting overall digestive health.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Rich in tannins and flavonoids, meadowsweet flowers help reduce inflammation and pain associated with hemorrhoids.
Leaves: High in fragrant oils, meadowsweet leaves can soothe irritation and promote healing of hemorrhoidal tissues.
Rhyzomes: Containing salicylic acid, meadowsweet rhyzomes can alleviate pain and reduce swelling in hemorrhoids.
remedy_recipe: In a pot, boil 2 cups of water and add 2 tablespoons of dried meadowsweet flowers.
Reduce heat and let it simmer for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the tea to remove the solid particles.
Allow the tea to cool down to a comfortable temperature.
Drink 2-3 cups of meadowsweet tea daily for hemorrhoid relief.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Alder buckthorn tea helps with hemorrhoids because it contains anthraquinones, compounds that have a laxative effect and can help to soften stools, making bowel movements less strained and reducing the pressure on hemorrhoids. The tea also has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce swelling and discomfort in the affected area. Additionally, alder buckthorn tea is rich in antioxidants, which can help to improve overall digestive health and reduce the risk of hemorrhoids. It's important to note that while alder buckthorn tea can be helpful in managing hemorrhoids, it should not be used as a substitute for medical treatment, especially in severe cases.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Alder buckthorn tea has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and pain in hemorrhoids, thanks to the presence of anthraquinones and flavonoids.
Laxative: The tea can help soften stools and increase bowel movements due to its laxative effect, which is attributed to anthraquinones like aloin and chrysophanol.
Astringent: Its astringent properties help tighten and tone tissues, reducing bleeding and promoting healing of hemorrhoids, due to tannins like gallic acid.

Antioxidant: The tea contains antioxidants that protect the body from damage caused by free radicals, helping to maintain healthy blood vessels and reduce inflammation, which is facilitated by the presence of phenolic acids and flavonoids.

Venotonic: Alder buckthorn tea can help improve venous tone and reduce venous insufficiency, which contributes to hemorrhoid development; this is achieved through the action of rutin and other flavonoids.
remedy_parts: Bark: The bark of the alder buckthorn tree is commonly used to make medicinal tea for hemorrhoids due to its anthraquinone content, which has laxative properties and can help relieve symptoms of hemorrhoids.

Leaves: The leaves of the alder buckthorn plant are used in making medicinal tea for hemorrhoids because of their anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling and pain associated with hemorrhoids.
Berries: The berries of the alder buckthorn plant contain anthraquinones, which can help stimulate the bowels and provide relief from constipation, a common cause of hemorrhoids.

Roots: The roots of the alder buckthorn plant are used in making medicinal tea for hemorrhoids due to their astringent properties, which can help reduce bleeding and promote healing of damaged tissues.
remedy_recipe: In a pot, combine 1 tablespoon of dried alder buckthorn bark with 8 ounces of cold water.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce heat and let it simmer for 15 minutes.
Remove from heat and add 1 teaspoon of dried chamomile flowers, letting it steep for an additional 5 minutes.
Strain the tea, discarding the solid plant material.
Drink the tea while it's still warm, 2-3 times a day, before meals.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Witch hazel tea helps with hemorrhoids because it contains tannins, which have anti-inflammatory and astringent properties. These properties can help reduce swelling, irritation, and pain in the affected area. Witch hazel may also help to strengthen blood vessels, which can prevent the recurrence of hemorrhoids. Additionally, witch hazel has been shown to have antioxidant properties, which can help to protect the skin and promote healing. Drinking witch hazel tea can provide relief from the symptoms of hemorrhoids, while also helping to treat the underlying cause of the condition.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Witch hazel tea has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and irritation in hemorrhoids, thanks to its tannins and gallic acid content.
Astringent: Its astringent properties help shrink and constrict swollen tissues, providing relief from hemorrhoid symptoms, due to the high tannin content.
Antioxidant: The rich antioxidant content of witch hazel tea, including flavonoids and proanthocyanidins, helps protect blood vessels and promote healing in hemorrhoids.
Analgesic: Witch hazel tea has pain-relieving properties that can alleviate the discomfort associated with hemorrhoids, attributed to its volatile oils and gallic acid.
Antimicrobial: Its antimicrobial properties help prevent infection in hemorrhoids, ensuring a faster recovery, due to the presence of tannins, volatile oils, and other phenolic compounds.
remedy_parts: Bark: The bark of the witch hazel tea plant contains tannins and oils that have astringent properties, making it effective in reducing inflammation and pain associated with hemorrhoids.
Leaves: The leaves of the plant are rich in flavonoids and volatile oils, which have anti-inflammatory and hemostatic properties, making them useful for treating hemorrhoids.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes of the plant contain high levels of phenolic acids and tannins, which have astringent and anti-inflammatory effects, making them beneficial for hemorrhoid relief.
Flowers: The flowers of the witch hazel tea plant are a source of flavonoids and essential oils, which have anti-inflammatory and soothing properties, making them useful for treating hemorrhoids.
remedy_recipe: In a pot, combine 1 quart of water and 2 tablespoons of dried witch hazel leaves, then bring the mixture to a boil.
Reduce heat, cover the pot, and let it simmer for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the tea to remove the leaves, then let it cool to a comfortable drinking temperature.
Stir in honey or lemon juice to taste, if desired.
Drink 1 cup of the tea, 3 times a day, for relief of hemorrhoid symptoms.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: St. John's wort tea helps with hemorrhoids because it has anti-inflammatory and astringent properties that can reduce swelling, pain, and bleeding in the affected area. The tea is made from the flowers, leaves, and stems of the St. John's wort plant, which contains several active compounds, including hypericin and hyperforin. These compounds have been shown to improve microcirculation, reduce inflammation, and promote healing. Drinking St. John's wort tea can help alleviate the symptoms of hemorrhoids and promote overall digestive health. However, it is important to note that St. John's wort can interact with certain medications, so it is always best to consult with a healthcare provider before starting any new herbal remedy.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: St. John's wort tea can reduce inflammation in hemorrhoids due to the presence of active constituents like hypericin and hyperforin. This property helps alleviate pain and swelling associated with hemorrhoids.
Antimicrobial: The tea has antimicrobial properties, which can help prevent infections in hemorrhoids. Hyperforin is responsible for this antimicrobial activity.
Analgesic: St. John's wort tea can help relieve pain caused by hemorrhoids, thanks to its analgesic properties. Hypericin and hyperforin are the active constituents that provide this relief.
Astringent: The tea has astringent properties that can help shrink and tighten tissues, reducing the size of hemorrhoids. This effect is due to the tannins present in St. John's wort.

Antioxidant: St. John's wort tea contains antioxidants that help protect blood vessels and reduce oxidative stress, which can contribute to hemorrhoid development and discomfort. Flavonoids and phenolic acids are the main antioxidants in the tea.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Contain high levels of hypericin and hyperforin, which have anti-inflammatory properties beneficial for hemorrhoids.
Leaves: Rich in flavonoids and tannins, which help reduce swelling and alleviate pain associated with hemorrhoids.
Buds: High in essential oils, providing soothing and healing effects for hemorrhoid-related discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Measure - Boil 1 cup of water and let it cool down to 212 degrees Fahrenheit.
Prepare - Add 2 teaspoons of dried St. John's Wort herb to a tea infuser or teapot.
Steep - Pour the hot water over the herb and let it steep for 10-15 minutes.
Strain - Remove the tea infuser or strain the tea into a cup.
Serve - Drink the tea while it's still warm, up to three times a day for hemorrhoid relief.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender tea helps with hemorrhoids because it has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce swelling and pain in the affected area. The soothing effects of lavender can also help alleviate irritation and promote healing. Additionally, lavender has been shown to improve circulation, which can help increase the flow of oxygen and nutrients to the affected area, further aiding in the healing process. Drinking lavender tea may also help relax the body and reduce stress, which can help alleviate some of the discomfort associated with hemorrhoids. Overall, lavender tea can be a natural and effective way to find relief from the symptoms of hemorrhoids.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: English lavender tea has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and pain in hemorrhoids, thanks to its active constituents like linalyl acetate and linalool.
Analgesic: It provides pain relief, which is crucial for individuals suffering from hemorrhoids, due to the presence of compounds such as cineole and geraniol.

Antispasmodic: The antispasmodic nature of English lavender tea helps relax smooth muscles, alleviating discomfort and promoting bowel movement regularity, attributed to active constituents like linalool and linalyl acetate.

Wound healing: Its wound healing properties aid in the recovery of damaged tissues, important for hemorrhoids as it can help accelerate the healing process, attributed to compounds like tannins and flavonoids.

Antimicrobial: The antimicrobial properties of English lavender tea help prevent infections, ensuring a speedy recovery and reducing the risk of complications, due to active constituents like eucalyptol and camphor.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, English lavender flowers help reduce irritation and promote healing for hemorrhoids.
Leaves: Containing fragrant oils and therapeutic properties, lavender leaves can soothe skin and alleviate pain and swelling associated with hemorrhoids.
Buds: High in tannins, lavender buds can help constrict blood vessels, reducing inflammation and discomfort in hemorrhoids.
remedy_recipe: In a pot, boil 1 cup of water and add 1 tablespoon of dried English lavender flowers, letting it steep for 10 minutes.
Strain the lavender tea to remove the flowers, leaving you with the infused liquid.
Allow the lavender tea to cool down to a warm temperature that is still comfortable to the touch.
Sit in a comfortable position and gently pour the lavender tea onto a clean cotton ball or pad.
Apply the soaked cotton ball or pad onto the affected hemorrhoid area for 10-15 minutes, repeating as needed up to 3 times a day.
